CONTENT.Honours: Napredak KruševacSerbian First League: 2012–13, 2015–16Red Star BelgradeSerbian SuperLiga: 2018–19, 2019–20
CONTENT.Club career: Vulić began playing football in his hometown, with the youth system of Napredak Kruševac before debuting for them professionally. On 5 May 2018, he made an assist and scored a goal in a 2-2 tie with Partizan. Over the course of the 2017–18 season, he scored a total of five goals in 30 games played and was also the team's captain.Vulić signed a three-year contract with Red Star Belgrade on 21 December 2018. On 18 September 2019, he made his UEFA Champions League debut when he came on as a substitute in a 3-0 loss to Bayern Munich.Vulić signed a three-year contract with Crotone on 5 September 2020.On 20 July 2022, Vulić moved to Perugia.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of end of 2021–22 season.As of 11 June 2021
CONTENT.International career: He made his debut for Serbia national football team on 7 June 2021 in a friendly against Jamaica.

SUMMARY: Miloš Vulić (Serbian Cyrillic: Милош Вулић; born 19 August 1996) is a Serbian professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for Serbian club TSC Bačka Topola.
